titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of TNA DL 25/546

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=868 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:24:08.47586+00 | translation moa=869 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:24:05.305431+00

p.cl:
Notum sit om ... et ... quod ... ona et ...red...
concedentibus here ... et conce...us ... in perpetuum ... elemosina pro animabus ... . s .
antecessorum et successorum ... de ... ...de skeldhou cum omnibus pertinentiis ... Marie ...
monachis de Man ... sic sicha diuidit Godefridi moram et molas . et sicut
riuus diuidit langliferee et terram de Mor ... sicut diuisa nota est omnibus comprouincialibus inter ...
nam et eandem terram de Mora et descendit in mare per eskeldhou . Hanc terram que infra has diuisas con
tra prefatis monachis concedimus et sigilli nostri munimine roboramus liberam et quietam ab omni terreno serui
cio quod ad nos pertinet in bosco et plano in pratis et pasturis . in moris et mosis et mariscis . in salinis
et molendinis . in uiis et semiti ... possum fieri aisementis tam nominatis quam non nominatis .
sicut sacrificium deo in odorem suauitatis saltem uespertinum pro animabus nostris et animabus patrum et matrum nostrarum ux
orum et filiorum nostrorum et omnium amicorum nostrorum tam uiuorum quam defunctorum  excepto quod monachi forensic
seruicium facient .

His Testibus . Rogero priore de furn’ . Iuone decano de Copeland’ . Adam persona de millum . Roberto presbitero de punzurebi . Willelmo de essebi . Iohanne filio pontii et Willelmo filio eius . Efwar
filio Vlfi et ketello fratre eius . Ric’ filio pontii . hugone filio efwardi . Alano filio ketelli . Willelmo
lugbriht .

Let it be known to all ... with the consent of our heirs ... in everlasting alms for the souls ... that is to say, of our ancestors and successors ... of Skeldhou with all its pertinents ... to St Mary and the monks of Man ... as the syke divides Godfrey's moor and the mills, and as the stream divides Langliferee and the land of Mor... as the boundary is known to all local inhabitants between ... and the same land of Mora and descends into the sea by the Skeldhou. This land which is within these boundaries we have granted to the aforementioned monks and strengthened by the defence of our seal, free and quit from every earthly service which pertains to us in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in moors, peat-bogs and marshes, in saltpans and mills, in roads and pathways ... can be done with easements as well named as unnamed, as an evening sacrifice to God as a savour of sweetness for our souls and the souls of our fathers and mothers, our wives and our children, and of all our friends, as well living as departed, except that the monks shall do forinsec service.
